title: Remove IPGT token error, make document upload optional on IP registration

requirements.text: In frontend/src/pages/RegisterIP.tsx, remove all remaining token balance checks, 'Insufficient IPGT tokens' error messages, and any related conditional logic that blocks or warns the user about token balance. The registration form must submit without any token-related validation or error states.

quotes: Remove the 'Insufficient IPGT tokens' error — strip any remaining token balance check or error message from the registration form and backend call

acceptanceCriteria: No 'Insufficient IPGT tokens' or equivalent token balance error message appears anywhere on the registration form or after submission
The form does not perform any token balance check before or during submission
All other form validation (title, category, jurisdiction) remains intact

requirements.text: In backend/main.mo, remove any token balance validation from the IP registration function (e.g. any check that the caller has sufficient IPGT balance before registering an IP). The registerIP function must accept valid registration requests without checking token balances.

quotes: Remove the 'Insufficient IPGT tokens' error — strip any remaining token balance check or error message from the registration form and backend call

acceptanceCriteria: The registerIP backend function does not return an error variant related to insufficient token balance
IP registration succeeds regardless of the caller's token balance
All other registration validations (duplicate hash check, required fields) remain intact

requirements.text: In frontend/src/pages/RegisterIP.tsx, make the file upload input optional. Remove any 'required' constraint, validation error, or submission guard that prevents the form from being submitted without an attached document. Users may optionally upload a PDF or image to auto-populate the document hash field, but it must not be required.

quotes: Optional document upload — file upload will no longer be required; users can submit the form without attaching a document

acceptanceCriteria: The file upload input does not have a 'required' attribute or equivalent validation constraint
Submitting the registration form without uploading a file does not produce a validation error
If a file is uploaded, the SHA-256 hash is still computed and populates the document hash field as before
If no file is uploaded and no manual hash is provided, the document hash field may remain empty and the form still submits successfully

constraints: Do not modify frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, or any file under frontend/src/components/ui
Do not alter any other page, component, or backend logic beyond what is specified in these requirements
The duplicate document hash uniqueness check in the backend must remain intact
nonGoals: Adding a random hash generator button (explicitly deferred from this build)
Persistent IP storage changes (not included in this scoped build)
Any visual or styling changes to RegisterIP.tsx beyond removing token-related UI
Changes to any other page, route, or component
